ops.title: 2026-07-14 hooks-brick outage's \"Edit tool strips trailing space\" root cause is FALSIFIED; real fault was an author-side replace_all; ccx code edit --match shipped as the verifiable lane
ops.body:
The 2026-07-14 outage (cc-context `.claude/settings.json` hooks glued into `uvx capt-hook runPreToolUse`/`runStop`, exit-2 on every PreToolUse and Stop — total session brick) was post-mortemed as "the built-in Edit tool strips a trailing space from new_string". That claim is **falsified**: live probes on 2026-07-15 showed Edit preserves trailing spaces byte-exact (end of new_string, interior line, and replace_all) and fails loudly on a whitespace-mismatched old_string instead of fuzzy-matching. Forensics found the outage session's transcript pruned from all 18 transcript roots (the failing tool_use is unrecoverable), no glued `runPreToolUse` token in any findable tool input, and the post-mortem's "I've written that up in memory" claim empty. Verdict: an author-side replace_all whose replacement never carried the `run <Event>` interior space (never-present or consumed-by-old_string), rationalized post hoc as a tool defect.

**Why:** a post-mortem written from inside a broken session confabulated a tool defect for an author error — both of its checkable claims failed verification.

**How to apply:** never cite "Edit strips trailing whitespace" as fact. Reproduce an incident's stated root cause before building on it (three probes took minutes and inverted the design). The shipped response is `ccx code edit --match` (post-v0.20.0; exact-byte needle, ambiguity errors listing `line#hash` candidate anchors, `--at` scoping, `--all` per-match stanzas, content written verbatim, report echoes every written line) — the verifiable edit lane for configs that can brick a session. Related: [[cc-context-architecture]], [[ccx-v015-native-find-overhaul]].
